articles.title: OPENAI'S MISSED OPT-OUT TOOL DEADLINE
articles.summary: OpenAI faced criticism for not delivering the promised opt-out tool by its deadline, raising concerns over user data practices and transparency.

articles.title: CODE ASSIST, GOOGLE'S ENTERPRISE-FOCUSED CODING ASSISTANT, GETS THIRD-PARTY TOOLS
articles.summary: Google announced third-party tool support for Gemini Code Assist, enhancing its AI code completion capabilities. The tools, available in private preview, connect Code Assist to external applications, increasing productivity and minimizing context switching. Initially, tools are limited to Google Cloud partners, with offerings from GitLab, GitHub, and others at launch.

articles.title: AN ANALYTIC THEORY OF CREATIVITY IN CONVOLUTIONAL DIFFUSION MODELS
articles.summary: A fascinating paper that explores closed form equations that can model generated images from diffusion models. This means that with a high degree of confidence you can predict the image that will be generated, in a simpler setting.

articles.title: CENTAUR: A FOUNDATION MODEL OF HUMAN COGNITION
articles.summary: Researchers have trained a language-like model that can predict cognitive reactions in human participants better than an MRI. This can be used to continue study in behavioral science and neuroscience.

articles.title: PARALLEL GENETIC ALGORITHM
articles.summary: This study suggests a method for reconstructing the genetic operation and designing a development framework for efficient parallel acceleration.

articles.title: DYNAMIC 3D GAUSSIAN TRACKING FOR GRAPH-BASED NEURAL DYNAMICS MODELING
articles.summary: This code uses advances in general 3D vision techniques to improve the niche of robot vision. Specifically, it can predict the dynamics of objects being manipulated by a robotic arm which then in turn improves the system's overall manipulation performance.

articles.title: PROCESS REINFORCEMENT THROUGH IMPLICIT REWARDS
articles.summary: There are very few promising open replications of o1 reasoning. This work is quite promising in that it uses implicit rewards that don't require a formal reward method or model, but also rewards the outcome which is inline with what we know about reasoning models. The code is in progress, but its developers have released the data and models.

articles.title: SINGLE MODALITY 3D OBJECT DETECTION
articles.summary: This repository contains a 3D object detection framework designed for single modality inputs, particularly catering to simplified and efficient scenarios.

articles.title: A VIDEO-LANGUAGE ASSISTANT
articles.summary: A conditional diffusion model integrating visual and textual inputs designed to generate high-quality images conditioned on diverse text-visual contexts.

articles.title: HUMAN PREFERENCE LEARNING FOR VIDEO GENERATION
articles.summary: VisionReward is a fine-grained and multi-dimensional reward model.

articles.title: WONDERFUL MATRICES
articles.summary: A collection of diverse matrix operations implemented efficiently, useful for mathematical and scientific computing tasks.

articles.title: CHATTIME
articles.summary: An interactive chat-based application that integrates time-tracking features, simplifying task management for teams and individuals.

articles.title: VISION FOUNDATION MODEL FOR REMOTE SENSING
articles.summary: CrossEarth is the first vision foundation model aimed at generalizing across diverse remote sensing scenarios.
